must_detect.matched_text:
On 2026-05-28, payroll onboarding forms queued incorrectly due to a rule misconfiguration in the NeraCloud SG Pte Ltd integration; 23 internal employee records were delayed but not transmitted externally.
PDPA assessment: processing was within employment context; no sensitive biometric, health, or union data were involved; risk level “Low” and no breach notification threshold was met.
Cross-border transfer approval: Data Export Approval T-EX-2026-014 (closed) authorised transfer of salary bank codes and employee initials to NeraCloud SG Pte Ltd (Singapore) under DPA Schedule 2 with SCC‑equivalent clauses; export logs show restricted fields only.
One DSAR from a former intern was completed on 2026-05-20 with erasure of recruitment files; confirmation was sent via the role mailbox privacy@siammeridian.co.th and the ticket was closed.
No consent withdrawals related to payroll have been received during this period; existing consents for optional benefits remain valid as recorded in the HR portal.
Data minimisation and retention: onboarding forms collect job title, department, bank branch code, and masked account suffix (e.g., ****4321); the “National ID scan” field is disabled; retention tag EMP-ONBRD-12M is applied automatically.
MNPI screen: incident content does not include budgets, forecasts, M&A planning, or other information likely to affect BGX trading; a routine operational update was already published under BGX Rule 51 on 2026-05-29.
Security remediation: the workflow rule was corrected; access logs confirm only the HR_OPER role accessed the queue; vendor provided SOC 2 annex confirmation; no credentials or full bank numbers were exposed.
Employment and processor governance: HR continues under Policy HR-11 (PDPA Employment Data) and DPA Schedule 2 with NeraCloud SG Pte Ltd and KoalaPay APAC Sdn Bhd for payroll disbursement; SIAT-aligned cross-border terms remain in force.
Disclosure alignment: our Form BGX-56-1 One Report privacy note already lists processors and cross-border storage; Legal memo LG-PDPA-2026-07 concludes no amendment is required.
Erasure/rectification status: no outstanding erasure requests; rectification items in PDPA-REQ-000187 are marked “Resolved” with audit trail retained.
Communications hygiene: staff were reminded not to use phrases such as “material non-public information” in email subjects; this guidance does not indicate any handling of MNPI.
DSAR channel: requests may be filed via Company > Privacy Centre or the role mailbox dpo@siammeridian.co.th; physical address fields are not required for verification.
Closure: status “No Reportable Breach” under PDPA Section 37; a line-item summary will be included in the next quarterly compliance update to SIAT and BGX.
